title: Vanda lissochiloides
taxonomy: Vanda lissochiloides is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Vanda and is a type of epiphytic orchid. Vanda lissochiloides is native to Southeast Asia and is known for its unique characteristics. The species is often grown in tropical gardens and is prized for its vibrant flowers. In terms of its taxonomic classification, Vanda lissochiloides is a member of the subtribe Vανδae within the tribe Vandeae.

intro: Vanda lissochiloides is a type of orchid, specifically a species of the genus Vanda, known for its unique, delicate flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits of reducing inflammation and improving respiratory function. The bioactive constituents of Vanda lissochiloides include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Vanda lissochiloides include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory and digestive issues.
